name: New Delhi
summary: New Delhi, India's bustling capital, presents a mixed safety picture for tourists with significant scam risks but relatively low violent crime against visitors. <cite index="1-14,13-1,13-4">Violent crime is uncommon against foreign travelers, though incidents of murder, robbery, and rape have decreased in recent years according to Delhi Police statistics.</cite> The city's main challenges lie in petty crime and tourist-targeted scams, particularly around transport hubs and tourist areas.
quickVerdict: <cite index="3-15,6-2,6-5">The US State Department rates India at Level 2: Exercise Increased Caution due to risks of crime and terrorism, with some areas having higher risks.</cite> While Delhi can be challenging for first-time visitors, millions of tourists visit safely each year by taking proper precautions. The biggest risks are scams, petty theft, and air pollution rather than violent crime. <cite index="42-14,42-15">India is not particularly challenging due to crime, and crimes against tourists, including female tourists, are relatively rare.</cite> Smart travelers who stay alert, use reputable transportation, and avoid common scam scenarios can explore the city's rich cultural offerings safely.

neighborhoods.name: South Delhi (Hauz Khas, Khan Market)
neighborhoods.description: <cite index="45-13,45-14">The best areas to stay in for convenience and safety are South and Central Delhi, well serviced by the metro and close to attractions.</cite> <cite index="61-1,61-7">For nightlife, stick to places in South Delhi or Central Delhi that are known for being lively and secure, with areas like Hauz Khas and Khan Market being comfortable.</cite>

neighborhoods.name: Central Delhi (Connaught Place)
neighborhoods.description: Well-patrolled tourist hub with good infrastructure and metro connectivity. <cite index="70-1,70-19">Major tourist areas like Connaught Place are safe with good lighting and police presence.</cite> Watch for persistent touts but generally secure for visitors.

neighborhoods.name: New Delhi Railway Station Area
neighborhoods.description: Busy transport hub with heavy tourist traffic but significant scam activity. <cite index="31-23,40-12">Security guards and officials may be involved in elaborate scams directing tourists to fake travel agencies.</cite> Stay vigilant and pre-arrange transportation.

neighborhoods.name: Paharganj (Main Bazaar)
neighborhoods.description: <cite index="45-15,45-16">Many visitors end up in Paharganj, the backpacker area - do not stay in either Paharganj or Old Delhi.</cite> While budget-friendly, the area has poor infrastructure, aggressive touts, and higher crime rates. Solo female travelers should avoid.

neighborhoods.name: Old Delhi (Chandni Chowk)
neighborhoods.description: <cite index="34-2,34-15">The most common tourist friction is in Old Delhi — persistent touts near Chandni Chowk and the Red Fort.</cite> Extremely crowded, chaotic traffic, and frequent pickpocketing. Requires extra caution, especially for solo travelers.

neighborhoods.name: Outer Delhi/NCR Border Areas
neighborhoods.description: Remote areas with limited police presence and poor lighting. <cite index="48-8,66-9">Better to avoid outskirts at night, travel via trusted apps like Ola and Uber.</cite> Industrial zones and unauthorized colonies pose higher risks for tourists.

scams.name: Fake Government Tourist Office
scams.description: <cite index="31-28,32-10">Rickshaw drivers, taxi drivers and locals try to lure you into fake tourist agencies where they get commissions, often claiming your hotel is closed or the street is blocked due to protests.</cite>
scams.howToAvoid: <cite index="33-14">The only official government tourist office is at 88 Janpath near Connaught Lane.</cite> Never trust drivers who insist on taking you to 'tourist offices' and always verify hotel bookings independently.

scams.name: Airport Transport Scams
scams.description: <cite index="31-19,31-20,31-21">Taxi drivers from airport talk to fake policemen and tell you roads are blocked due to protests, COVID measures, or other reasons, then take you to fake travel agencies.</cite>
scams.howToAvoid: Use prepaid taxi counters, Delhi Metro Airport Express, or book rides through verified apps like Uber/Ola. Pre-arrange hotel pickup when possible and never believe claims about blocked roads or closed hotels.

scams.name: Railway Station Ticket Scams
scams.description: <cite index="35-25,35-26,35-29">People dressed as officials claim your ticket needs stamping for validation or that the reservation center is closed due to riots, ants, or other absurd excuses.</cite>
scams.howToAvoid: <cite index="35-27,35-28">Confirmed tickets only need to be shown to the train conductor with your passport - you don't need to pay for anything else after purchasing your ticket.</cite>

scams.name: Helpful Baggage Carriers
scams.description: <cite index="35-8,35-9,35-11">At airports, men grab your bags before you can object, claiming to help, then demand payment.</cite>
scams.howToAvoid: <cite index="35-13,35-14,35-15">Keep hold of your bags at all times. If you need help, use official porters in red shirts, but only if prepared to pay.</cite>

soloFemale.overview: <cite index="41-1,41-21">Delhi is safe for solo female travellers as long as you use common sense and stick to some rules.</cite> <cite index="1-47,1-48">The U.S. government advises women traveling alone to observe strict security precautions and use women-only public transport available in Delhi.</cite> While harassment like staring and following is common, serious incidents are relatively rare. <cite index="42-32,42-33,42-34">Most encounters with men are annoying rather than threatening - groping in crowded areas and following on streets, but they rarely cross serious lines.</cite>
tips: <cite index="41-14,41-15,41-18">Dress modestly covering shoulders and legs, especially in Old Delhi, as revealing clothes attract unwanted attention.</cite>
<cite index="45-20,45-21">Use the women's coach on Delhi Metro (first coach in moving direction) - it's less crowded and you won't get stared at.</cite>
<cite index="41-29,41-30,41-31,41-32">Use the Ola app for booking rickshaws and taxis for fair prices and safety tracking, especially for evening/night travel.</cite>

nightSafety.overview: <cite index="61-16,61-17,61-18">Delhi safety at night varies by area - major tourist zones like India Gate and Connaught Place are well-lit with police presence, but avoid isolated areas.</cite> <cite index="66-1,66-2">For solo travelers, stick to daytime and before 10pm ideally, though moving between safe locations later via cabs is manageable.</cite> <cite index="65-1,65-14,65-16">Safest nightlife spots include Cyber Hub, Aerocity, Hauz Khas, and Saket Select Citywalk.</cite>
tips: <cite index="65-2,65-17">Never leave drinks unattended, travel with friends when possible, and share your location with family.</cite>
<cite index="65-18">Book cabs via verified apps like Uber, Ola, and Rapido for their safety tracking features and verified drivers.</cite>
<cite index="45-2,45-23">Delhi Metro is safe until about 8pm for solo travelers, after that it's better to take an Uber.</cite>

transport.metro: <cite index="45-17,45-18,45-19">Often called the lifeline of the city, the metro is modern, clean, efficient, inexpensive and very easy to use with 12 lines covering 400km, all in English and Hindi with color coding.</cite> <cite index="50-4,50-5">The Delhi Metro is reliable and safe with dedicated women-only coaches, particularly helpful during rush hours.</cite>
transport.rideshare: <cite index="55-25,55-36">Ola offers real-time driver monitoring and SOS button for emergencies, with users prioritizing apps that show exact fares upfront and have safety features like driver verification.</cite> <cite index="53-26,53-27">Uber prioritizes safety with in-app emergency assistance button to call authorities if needed.</cite>
transport.taxis: <cite index="68-24,68-25,68-26">Taxi drivers often take advantage of tourists with fast meters or wrong routes, so take only official taxis. Some unlicensed taxis have taken people to wrong hotels.</cite> <cite index="51-7,58-10">New partnerships between Delhi Metro and taxi cooperatives aim to provide affordable, reliable and safe last-mile connectivity services.</cite>
transport.tips: <cite index="34-8">Use ride-hailing apps like Uber and Ola instead of unmarked taxis — they have GPS tracking and fare transparency built in.</cite> <cite index="34-16">Use the Delhi Metro wherever possible (safe, air-conditioned, cheap) and book airport transfers through pre-paid apps.</cite>

customs: <cite index="81-1,81-2">Greet with 'Namaste' or 'Namaskar' by placing palms together and bowing slightly, especially when meeting older people.</cite>
<cite index="84-23,84-24">Eat with your right hand only - the left hand is for unsavory functions while the right hand is for eating and greeting.</cite>
<cite index="86-1,86-2,87-15">Remove shoes when entering temples, homes, and some religious sites to prevent dirt from coming into sanctified environments.</cite>

health.overview: <cite index="22-4,22-7">Delhi faces a serious air pollution crisis with PM2.5 levels creating a public health emergency. Between 2022-2024, 204,758 cases of Acute Respiratory Infection were reported to emergency departments, with 30,424 requiring hospitalization.</cite> <cite index="25-4,27-4,27-21">Current Air Quality Index levels are around 153-159 (Unhealthy), requiring extra precautions especially for those with respiratory conditions.</cite>
health.water: <cite index="42-18,42-19">Travelers' diarrhea (Delhi belly) is the single biggest problem travelers face, with bad water being the main cause rather than food poisoning.</cite> Always drink bottled or properly treated water and avoid ice in drinks.
health.vaccinations: <cite index="42-4,42-5,42-7,42-8">Be very careful of mosquitoes due to dengue fever risk, which has no vaccine. Malaria is less of a risk, but use DEET repellent liberally.</cite>
health.altitude: <cite index="28-4,28-6">Air pollution is causing noticeable rise in allergies, sinus issues, respiratory infections, asthma, and COPD across all age groups, especially affecting children and elderly.</cite> Consider air purifiers for accommodation and limit outdoor activities during high pollution days.

emergency.general: <cite index="71-5,71-10">112 (unified emergency number launched in 2026 for all services)</cite>
emergency.usEmbassy: US Embassy New Delhi: +91-11-2419-8000. Located in Chanakyapuri diplomatic enclave, provides 24/7 emergency services for US citizens including passport replacement, emergency loans, and crime reporting assistance.

faq.q: Is New Delhi safe for tourists?
faq.a: <cite index="63-6,63-7">Delhi does have a high crime rate compared to the rest of India, but crimes against tourists are rare. The city is safe as long as you are careful.</cite> The main risks are scams and petty crime rather than violent incidents. Millions of tourists visit annually without problems.

faq.q: Is New Delhi safe at night?
faq.a: <cite index="61-16,61-17">Parts of Delhi are safe at night - major tourist areas have good lighting and police presence, but avoid isolated areas.</cite> <cite index="70-1,70-2">Stick to well-lit areas like India Gate, Connaught Place, and use metered taxis or app-based cabs.</cite>

faq.q: Is New Delhi safe for solo female travelers?
faq.a: <cite index="47-1,47-26">After traveling India alone for over 10 months, the general impression is that India is safe for solo female travel as long as you use common sense.</cite> <cite index="1-48,45-20">Use women-only metro coaches and public transport designated for women, available as a free service in Delhi.</cite>

faq.q: What areas should I avoid in New Delhi?
faq.a: <cite index="45-15,45-16">Avoid staying in Paharganj (backpacker area) and Old Delhi.</cite> <cite index="48-8,66-9">Avoid outskirts at night and always travel via trusted apps like Ola and Uber in remote areas.</cite> Be extra cautious around transport hubs where scam activity is highest.

faq.q: Is it safe to use public transport in New Delhi?
faq.a: <cite index="51-17,51-18">Delhi Metro has emerged as the backbone of public transportation, providing fast, safe and reliable mass transit for millions daily.</cite> <cite index="50-4,50-5">The metro is reliable and safe with dedicated women-only coaches.</cite> Avoid city buses which can be crowded and less safe.
